(a) If the department suspends a person’s license under Section 521.292 (Department’s Determination for License Suspension) or revokes a person’s license under Section 521.294 (Department’s Determination for License Revocation) , the department shall send a notice of suspension or revocation by:

(1) first class mail to the person’s address in the records of the department; or

(2) e-mail if the person has provided an e-mail address to the department and has elected to receive notice electronically.

(b) Notice is considered received on the fifth day after the date the notice is sent.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 521. Driver's Licenses & Certificates
##### § 521.295 — Notice of Department’s Determination

(a) If the department suspends a person’s license under Section 521.292 (Department’s Determination for License Suspension) or revokes a person’s license under Section 521.294 (Department’s Determination for License Revocation) , the department shall send a notice of suspension or revocation by:

    (1) first class mail to the person’s address in the records of the department; or

    (2) e-mail if the person has provided an e-mail address to the department and has elected to receive notice electronically.

(b) Notice is considered received on the fifth day after the date the notice is sent.

*Acts 2017, 85th Leg., R.S., Ch. 1079 (H.B.3376), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2017.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: If the department suspends a person’s license under Section 521.292 (Department’s Determination for License Suspension) or revokes a person’s license under Section 521.294 (Department’s Determination for License Revocation) , the department shall send a notice of suspension or revocation by:
first class mail to the person’s address in the records of the department; or
e-mail if the person has provided an e-mail address to the department and has elected to receive notice electronically.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Notice is considered received on the fifth day after the date the notice is sent.

history.raw: Acts 2017, 85th Leg., R.S., Ch. 1079 (H.B.3376), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2017.
